health_zone_table.basis: SitRep53 Table 2 (2026-07-06) transcribed + reconciled against the INSP PDF (post 25104, pdf sha256 d8d21e9c003d1ae7b3b50bdb99c5d1e516ec5bd3e2580ff3f92a54a23c694c9a, 13 pages), byte-verified against the live INSP upload.

reconciliation.note: Rows reconcile to official province/national totals: 1691 named confirmed cases plus 17 Ituri cases in zones not yet identified = 1708; all 580 confirmed deaths are named-zone attributed. The residual row is retained as unattributed context and excluded from mappable named-zone attribution.

lab_indicators_24h.note: National aggregate: Ituri 172 analyzed / 79 positive (45.9%); Nord-Kivu 77 analyzed / 5 positive (6.5%); Sud-Kivu 3 collected and under analysis (excluded from positivity). Positivity uses 84 positives over 249 analyzed samples.

care_continuity: SitRep53 Tableau 7: 680 in isolation at 84.1% occupancy (split promoted as 218 confirmed / 462 suspected to reconcile to the 680 census; the PDF printed 470 suspected, which would total 688). Nord-Kivu isolation at 115.0% saturation. A criminal fire struck CTE Kitatumba (Nord-Kivu). 7 recoveries (all Ituri) and 9 deaths among previously confirmed CTE patients (Ituri 5, Nord-Kivu 4).
A confirmed case escaped from CT Musienene before transfer to CTE Katwa (Nord-Kivu).
highlights: 84 new confirmed cases and 59 deaths (84.7% community) were reported for 2026-07-06, in Ituri (79 cases, 52 deaths) and Nord-Kivu (5 cases, 7 deaths). Confirmed deaths rose to 580; the banner CFR is 34.0%.
A new affected health zone (Boga, Ituri) was reported; affected zones rise to 37/104. Sud-Kivu has recorded no confirmed case since 2026-05-26.
The provider strike continues and expanded to Bunia and Rwampara (Ituri). Nord-Kivu CTE occupancy is 115% and a criminal fire hit CTE Kitatumba.
Ituri concentrates 91.1% of cases (n=1556) and 84.7% of deaths (n=491), CFR 31.6%. Bunia (472), Rwampara (357), Mongbwalu (298), Nyankunde (96) and Nizi (84) remain the most affected zones.
Nord-Kivu holds the highest provincial CFR at 59.1%, led by Katwa (73.2%), Oicha (66.7%) and Beni (65.5%).
laboratory: SitRep53 reports Ituri collected/analyzed 172 samples with 79 positive (45.9%).
Nord-Kivu collected/analyzed 77 samples with 5 positive (6.5%). Sud-Kivu collected 3 samples under analysis.
publication_context: INSP SitRep 53 reports through 2026-07-06 (post 25104; WP publish 2026-07-07T23:43:50Z). The report date is 06 July 2026.
SitRep53 is the current/latest operational snapshot; the cumulative rose by 84 confirmed and 59 deaths over 2026-07-06 vs SitRep52.
The outbreak is reported across 3 provinces and 37 of 104 health zones; Boga (Ituri) is newly affected.
Ituri remains the epicenter with 1556 of 1708 DRC confirmed cases (91.1%) and 491 deaths (CFR 31.6%).
redactions: Page contact details and operational names, if present outside table facts, are intentionally not promoted into public evidence text.
surveillance: 1216 alerts of the day were reported (66 J-1 + 1150 new) and 963 investigated (79.2%); Ituri 46.8% (223/476), Nord-Kivu 100.0% (726/726), Sud-Kivu 100.0% (14/14). The PDF renders the investigated total as '9 63' (spacing); context supports 963.
237 suspected cases of the day were validated (167 living, 70 community deaths); Ituri 170, Nord-Kivu 64, Sud-Kivu 3.
12190 contacts were under follow-up and 9167 seen (75.2%; PDF narrative typo '752%'); Ituri 73.0% (completeness 75%, Aru/Damas/Kambala/Mandima missing), Nord-Kivu 82.7%.
At PoE/PoC, 3 dead bodies were intercepted (Ituri 1 at PoC Mudzibala, Nord-Kivu 2 at PoC Kasitu/Mukulia) and all swabbed (100%).

alerts_total.data_gap_note: Report (J-1) 66; new alerts 1150; total alerts of the day 1216. Tableau 3 investigated 963 (79.2%). PDF renders the investigated total as '9 63' (spacing) and the follow-up narrative as '752%' (typo); table/headline support 963 and 75.2%.

challenges.finding: Community reluctance toward post-mortem sampling (EDS/swabs) and resistance delay confirmation, underestimate cases, and disrupt key activities.
challenges.response: Intensify RCCE, involve religious and community leaders, and reinforce team security.

challenges.finding: Insufficient CTE capacity with Nord-Kivu saturation cited at 138.8% in the challenge table; the banner reports national isolation occupancy 89.4% (628 patients), raising nosocomial-transmission risk.
challenges.response: Accelerate CTE construction/expansion and deploy additional temporary isolation structures.

challenges.finding: Contact follow-up performance reached 81.6% (challenge table cites 81.3%) versus the 95% target, leaving transmission chains uninterrupted.
challenges.response: Train, deploy, and motivate community relays (RECO) with reinforced supervision.

challenges.finding: Weak alert reporting in some affected health zones risks undetected, silent transmission.
challenges.response: Reinforce community-based surveillance and rapid alert investigation.

challenges.finding: Seventeen Ituri confirmed cases remain not yet ventilated by health zone, leaving gaps in transmission-chain reconstruction.
challenges.response: Conduct data reconciliation, prune duplicates, and reclassify/localize with active field verification.

challenges.finding: Insufficient supply of essential medicines (MEG) and medical inputs weakens holistic case management.
challenges.response: Advocacy and urgent procurement of essential medicines.

challenges.finding: Insufficient IPC inputs (PPE, chlorine) and limited training raise nosocomial-infection risk; the challenge table reports 96 health-worker infections and 19 deaths (CFR 19.7%).
challenges.response: Urgent procurement and reinforced IPC training.

challenges.finding: A backlog of un-analyzed samples in Nord-Kivu and late diagnosis persist amid a 54.2% provincial CFR (challenge table).
challenges.response: Deploy a laboratory catch-up plan and reinforce diagnostic capacity.

challenges.finding: Insufficient ambulances and isolation structures (a gap of about 20 centers) delay transfer and isolation and raise transmission risk.
challenges.response: Urgent deployment of logistics and additional infrastructure.

challenges.finding: Insufficient coordination and weak use of COUSP frameworks cause operational inefficiency and delayed decisions.
challenges.response: Reinforce coordination mechanisms at all levels and operationalize provincial COUSP.

challenges.finding: A financial-resource gap of about USD 20 million limits implementation across all response pillars.
challenges.response: Urgent resource mobilization and partner advocacy.

challenges.finding: Insecurity and limited access (CODECO, ADF) restrict operations and access to high-risk zones.
challenges.response: Strengthen security coordination and humanitarian access.

challenges.finding: Compromised continuity of essential (non-Ebola) health services raises indirect mortality.
challenges.response: Effectively implement free care and reinforce continuity of essential health services (CEHS).

challenges.finding: High population mobility raises the risk of rapid geographic expansion; the Wamba/Haut-Uele imported cases stay counted within Nia-Nia (Ituri), and this cycle 2 dead bodies were intercepted at Ituri PoE/PoC.
challenges.response: Reinforce PoE/PoC surveillance and cross-border/inter-provincial coordination.

contacts_total.data_gap_note: Tableau 4 (06 July): 12190 under follow-up, 9167 seen, 75.2% (PDF narrative typo '752%'). Ituri completeness 75%; ZS Aru, Damas, Kambala, Mandima had no contact data at consolidation.

patient_movement_total.data_gap_note: SitRep53 Tableau 7 census 680 at 84.1% occupancy (Ituri 508 @84.0%, Nord-Kivu 148 @115.0%, Sud-Kivu 24 @53.3%). The 680 census is corroborated by the banner and the Tableau-7 province rows (508+148+24=680); the printed split '218 confirmed / 470 suspected' sums to 688 (internal source error), so the promoted suspected split is reconciled to 462 (=680-218) with the printed 470 retained as a conflict. Exits: 7 recoveries (all Ituri); 9 deaths among previously confirmed CTE patients (Ituri 5, Nord-Kivu 4).

priorities.priority: Resolve the 17 unventilated Ituri confirmed cases without distributing them to named health zones; keep the Wamba/Haut-Uele imported cases counted within Nia-Nia

Relieve isolation load at 89.4% national occupancy and the Nord-Kivu 57.4% CFR; close the reported gap of about 20 isolation centers, accelerate CTE construction/expansion (Mongbwalu +50 beds, HGR Bunia +60 beds, CTE Elikya), and sustain the clinical trial launched at CTE CME Rwampara
Improve contact follow-up (81.6% vs 95% target), reinforce intervention teams in affected ZS, clear the Nord-Kivu laboratory backlog, and protect IPC/EDS teams after the CMC Kiharo attack

preserved_conflicts: SitRep53 headline is DRC-only (1708 confirmed / 580 confirmed deaths across Ituri, Nord-Kivu, Sud-Kivu); the country-scope snapshot adds the Uganda component (20/2/11) separately.

CONFLICT (preserved): SitRep53 Tableau 2 prints the Nord-Kivu SUBTOTAL as 144 confirmed / 81 deaths (stale), but the Nord-Kivu zone rows sum to 149 / 88 and Table 1 / the narrative give 149 / 88. The promoted Nord-Kivu province total is 149 / 88 (zone-row and Table-1 concordant); the 144 / 81 printed subtotal is retained as a source presentation error.
SitRep53 supersedes SitRep52 (single report-day advance): +84 confirmed and +59 confirmed deaths for 2026-07-06. Ituri +79 (52 deaths), Nord-Kivu +5 (7 deaths).
SitRep53 carries an Ituri residual of 17 confirmed cases in zones not yet identified; public but not mappable to named health zones.

A new affected health zone (Boga, Ituri) was reported this cycle; affected-zone count rises to 37 of 104. The PDF header duplicates 'Boga' in the Ituri health-zone list; retained as a source presentation issue while using the unique-zone count.

CONFLICT (preserved): SitRep53 Tableau 7 census 680 (banner and province rows Ituri 508 + Nord-Kivu 148 + Sud-Kivu 24 = 680 concordant) at 84.1% occupancy, but the printed confirmed/suspected split '218 / 470' sums to 688. The promoted split is reconciled to the corroborated 680 census as 218 confirmed / 462 suspected; the printed 470 is retained here as a source presentation error. Nord-Kivu isolation at 115.0% saturation; a criminal fire struck CTE Kitatumba and a confirmed case escaped CT Musienene before transfer to CTE Katwa.
PDF source-presentation typos preserved: alert investigations render as '9 63' (=963); the contact follow-up narrative says '752%' (=75.2%).

Uganda anchor remains 20 cumulative confirmed / 2 deaths / 11 recoveries (Uganda MoH, last reviewed 2026-06-21); carried forward as INSP reports DRC only, so country-scope is 1728 confirmed / 582 deaths / 291 recovered.
Sud-Kivu has recorded no new confirmed case since 2026-05-26; it remains at 3 confirmed / 1 death (Miti-Murhesa).

The provider strike expanded to Bunia and Rwampara (Ituri). Several low-denominator zones report 100% case-fatality (Drodro 3/3, Kambala 2/2, Vuhovi 1/1, Lolwa 1/1, Boga 1/1); unstable small-number ratios.

review_notes: SitRep #053 (report date 2026-07-06; INSP post 25104; WP publish 2026-07-07T23:43:50Z). PDF sha256 d8d21e9c003d1ae7b3b50bdb99c5d1e516ec5bd3e2580ff3f92a54a23c694c9a, 13 pages, byte-verified against the live INSP upload.

SitRep53 supersedes SitRep52 (single report-day). DRC headline 1708 confirmed / 580 confirmed deaths (CFR 34.0%); +84 confirmed and +59 confirmed deaths vs SitRep52. On 06 July, 84 new confirmed (Ituri 79, Nord-Kivu 5) carried 84.7% community deaths.
CONFLICT resolved to zone-row + Table-1 concordance: Nord-Kivu promoted at 149 confirmed / 88 deaths; the Tableau 2 printed subtotal 144 / 81 is a stale source error retained in preserved_conflicts.
New affected health zone Boga (Ituri); affected-zone count 37 of 104. Header duplicates Boga (presentation issue). New map geometry/centroid required for Boga.
Health-zone rows reconcile to 1708 confirmed / 580 deaths: 1691 named confirmed cases plus 17 Ituri cases awaiting ventilation, all 580 confirmed deaths named-zone attributed.

Fresh Tableau 7 patient-movement: 680 in isolation (218 confirmed / 470 suspected) at 84.1%; Nord-Kivu 115.0% saturation. Recovered advanced to 280 DRC cumulative. Criminal fire at CTE Kitatumba; a confirmed case escaped CT Musienene before transfer to CTE Katwa.
Laboratory: Ituri 172 analyzed / 79 positive (45.9%); Nord-Kivu 77 analyzed / 5 positive (6.5%); Sud-Kivu 3 under analysis (249 analyzed / 84 positive overall, 33.7%).

Uganda anchor carried at 20 / 2 / 11 (last reviewed 2026-06-21); country-scope 1728 / 582 / 291. Provider strike expanded to Bunia and Rwampara; funding gap remains ~USD 20M; HCW infections 96 / 19 deaths (CFR 19.7%).
